response:
Sec. 1  
[THE WRITER IS CONSIDERING TWO PUBLISHERS BASED ON THEIR OFFERS FOR HER BOOK.]  

Sec. 2  
[PUBLISHER A OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF 3000 DOLLARS PLUS 5 PERCENT OF THE BOOKS SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 3  
[PUBLISHER B OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF 1500 DOLLARS PLUS 10 PERCENT OF THE BOOKS SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 4  
[TO DETERMINE WHEN PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE, WE NEED TO SET UP AN INEQUALITY.]  

Sec. 5  
[THE INEQUALITY IS 3000 PLUS 0.05 TIMES X GREATER THAN 1500 PLUS 0.10 TIMES X.]  

Sec. 6  
[SOLVING THIS INEQUALITY WILL GIVE THE RANGE OF X WHERE PUBLISHER A IS BETTER.]  

Sec. 7  
[WHEN THE SALES REVENUE IS BETWEEN 10000 AND 40000 DOLLARS, PUBLISHER A IS BETTER FOR X LESS THAN 30000 DOLLARS.]  

Sec. 8  
[THUS, THE WRITER SHOULD CHOOSE PUBLISHER A IF SALES ARE EXPECTED TO BE LESS THAN 30000 DOLLARS.]
